mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of periods when PAYEMS growth exceeds 100k units, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) reflect strong demand for residential construction, which correlates with higher corporate investment and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX records negative 20d returns in > 50% of months with HOUST growth above 2% despite the signal, or IC < 0.03 in 5-year backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal overheating supply chains and monetary policy tightening, which compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO and CPIAUCSL both rise by >1% month-over-month, or interaction effect IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 2% triggers a monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX records positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2%, or t-test p-value > 0.1 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market tightness regime (UNRATE < 5%) drives wage inflation and central bank rate hikes, which depress S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 50% of periods with UNRATE below 5%, or IC < 0.04 in 5-year backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates expanding demand that boosts corporate profits and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX records negative 60d returns in > 50% of quarters with GDPC1 growth above 3%, or IC < 0.03 in 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5.5% signals weakening labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 50% of periods with UNRATE above 5.5%, or t-test p-value > 0.1 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer and residential demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through increased corporate revenues.
failure_condition: SPX records negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS and HOUST both rise by >1% month-over-month, or interaction effect IC < 0.02.
